title: `_suggestions` module missing on Windows, untested
author: devdanzin
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:

The `_suggestions` module isn't available in Windows builds because it's missing from `PC/config.c`. Since it's not tested and only imported in `traceback.py` in a `try:` block guarding against `ImportError`, it's easy to not detect that it's missing.

If there is no objection, I'll create a PR adding the `_suggestions` module to `PC/config.c` (and perhaps `config_minimal.c`) plus basic tests to detect whether it's available and working as intended.

### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Windows

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-131759
* gh-131790
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Oh right, it should be available on Windows as well. Don't we have functional tests for this feature in test_traceback?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
>Don't we have functional tests for this feature in test_traceback?

We do, but `traceback.py` has a Python implementation that is automatically used if `_suggestions` fails to import:

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/f1967e72498209e42f7cf5eeff0cd84d1ec10d18/Lib/traceback.py#L1538-L1550

comments.author: zooba
comments.body:
How significant is this module's size/load time/impact?

Since it's only ever going to be used for interactive mode, I think I'd prefer it to be its own extension module, so that we don't pay any cost for it in normal use.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
> Since it's only ever going to be used for interactive mode

I think suggestions are provided for non-interactive use too, aren't they?


> I think I'd prefer it to be its own extension module

That'd probably be above my skill level, so if anyone wants to tackle this feel free to.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
Yes, suggestions are also used when running a script.

`demo.py`:

```
demo = 1
print(dmo)
```

Python suggests `Did you mean: 'demo'?`:

```
$ ./python demo.py 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/vstinner/python/main/demo.py", line 2, in <module>
    print(dmo)
          ^^^
NameError: name 'dmo' is not defined. Did you mean: 'demo'?
```

comments.author: zooba
comments.body:
It's still not a requirement for production code, so not having it loaded until it's needed would be better.

That said, I just looked at the size of the module and it's tiny. So no concerns about packing it into the main DLL.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> That said, I just looked at the size of the module and it's tiny. So no concerns about packing it into the main DLL.

@devdanzin: Do you feel able to make this change? Or do you want me to have a look?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: If you tackle this one, I'll use the time to try to fix up my in-flight PRs that I've been too slow to update, thanks!

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: I wrote https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/131759 to build the `_suggestions` extension on Windows.
